title: Ricinocarpus riedelianus
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus riedelianus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of Sapindales, an order of flowering plants. Within the Sapindaceae family, Ricinocarpus riedelianus is further grouped alongside other species that share similar characteristics. The classification of Ricinocarpus riedelianus is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. The plant's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other members of the Sapindaceae family.

intro: Ricinocarpus riedelianus, commonly known as the "Riedelian's Ricinocarpus", is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Brazil. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a possible treatment for various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Ricinocarpus riedelianus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and topical creams, with the most common preparations being infusions for skin conditions and creams for wound healing.
